Package nonprofitbookkeeping.service

Class FundAccountingService

java.lang.Object
nonprofitbookkeeping.service.FundAccountingService
public class FundAccountingService extends Object
Service class for managing fund accounting operations. This includes managing funds and accounts, their relationships, retrieving fund balances, and performing fund transfers. Funds and accounts are stored in in-memory maps.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructor
    Description

    Constructs a new FundAccountingService.

  • Constructor Details

    • FundAccountingService

      public FundAccountingService()
      Constructs a new FundAccountingService. Initializes empty maps for storing funds and accounts.

  • Method Details

    • addFund

      public void addFund(Fund fund)
      Adds a new fund to the service. The fund is stored using its name as the key.
      Parameters:
      fund - The Fund to add. Must not be null.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if a fund with the same name already exists, or if fund or its name is null.

    • removeFund

      public boolean removeFund(String fundName)
      Removes a fund from the service by its name. This method also ensures that the removed fund is disassociated from any accounts it was previously linked with by calling Account#removeFund(Fund).
      Parameters:
      fundName - The name of the fund to remove.
      Returns:
      true if the fund was found and removed, false otherwise.

    • addAccount

      public void addAccount(Account account)
      Adds a new account to the service. The account is stored using its name as the key.
      Parameters:
      account - The Account to add. Must not be null.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if an account with the same name already exists, or if account or its name is null.

    • removeAccount

      public boolean removeAccount(String accountName)
      Removes an account from the service by its name. This method also ensures that the removed account is disassociated from any funds it was previously linked with by calling Fund#removeAccount(Account).
      Parameters:
      accountName - The name of the account to remove.
      Returns:
      true if the account was found and removed, false otherwise.

    • listFunds

      public List<Fund> listFunds()
      Retrieves a list of all funds currently managed by this service.
      Returns:
      A new ArrayList containing all Fund objects. This is a copy, so modifications to the returned list will not affect internal storage.

    • saveFunds

      public void saveFunds(File companyDirectory) throws IOException
      Saves all funds to the persistent document store.
      Parameters:
      companyDirectory - retained for backwards compatibility but ignored by the method
      Throws:
      IOException - if writing to the database fails

    • loadFunds

      public void loadFunds(File companyDirectory) throws IOException
      Loads funds from the persistent document store. Existing in-memory funds are cleared before loading new ones.
      Parameters:
      companyDirectory - retained for backwards compatibility but ignored by the method
      Throws:
      IOException - if reading from the database fails

    • listAccounts

      public List<Account> listAccounts()
      Retrieves a list of all accounts currently managed by this service.
      Returns:
      A new ArrayList containing all Account objects. This is a copy, so modifications to the returned list will not affect internal storage.

    • getFundBalances

      public Map<String,BigDecimal> getFundBalances()
      Gets the current balances for all funds. The balance for each fund is obtained via Fund.getBalance().
      Returns:
      A Map where keys are fund names (String) and values are their corresponding balances (BigDecimal).

    • transferFunds

      public void transferFunds(String fromFund, String toFund, BigDecimal amount)
      Transfers a specified amount between two funds. This method directly adjusts the balances of the source and destination funds using Fund.setBalance(BigDecimal). It does not create accounting transactions.
      Parameters:
      fromFund - The name of the fund from which the amount will be transferred.
      toFund - The name of the fund to which the amount will be transferred.
      amount - The BigDecimal amount to transfer. Must be greater than zero.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if amount is not greater than zero, or if either fromFund or toFund does not exist.
